Title: Multifaceted Regulations of Gateway Enzyme Phenylalanine Ammonia-Lyase in the Biosynthesis of Phenylpropanoids

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A calcium-dependent protein kinase from Arabidopsis can phosphorylate a PAL peptide and poplar recombinant PAL in vitro. 2. This suggests possible phosphorylation-based post-translational regulation of PAL. 3. However, later phosphoproteomics did not confirm PAL phosphorylation in poplar, so physiological significance remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a PAL-modifying factor, but evidence is indirect/uncertain in this review.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited due to uncertain in planta relevance for PAL regulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Poplar phosphoproteomics referenced; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially modulates PAL activity via phosphorylation, but not established conclusively

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H is the immediate downstream enzyme metabolizing trans-cinnamic acid and is central to PAL feedback behavior. 2. Reducing C4H causes trans-cinnamate accumulation and lower PAL activity, supporting a feedback loop at pathway entry. 3. C4H also physically associates with PAL and other early phenylpropanoid enzymes in ER-associated metabolons.
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important for interpreting PAL regulation but not the main review focus compared with PAL.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-engineered with PAL to avoid trans-cinnamate buildup and feedback inhibition; useful for tuning lignin/flavonoid flux.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-suppression/overexpression studies; co-localization; membrane complex assays
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls trans-cinnamate turnover, affects PAL feedback regulation, and promotes downstream phenylpropanoid synthesis

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtUGT78D1 participates in flavonol 3-O-glycosylation. 2. In the ugt78d1 ugt78d2 double mutant, PAL1/PAL2 transcript levels and PAL activity are repressed. 3. This supports feedback inhibition of PAL by flavonol aglycone-related signals when flavonol glycosylation is compromised.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly on PAL through branch-pathway metabolite feedback rather than direct PAL regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of flavonol glycosylation may strongly affect feedback on upstream pathway genes; use with caution in flavonoid engineering.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis; transcript and enzyme activity assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Compromised activity suppresses flavonol synthesis and indirectly represses PAL1/PAL2 and CHS

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtUGT78D2 functions with UGT78D1 in flavonol 3-O-glycosylation. 2. Loss of both genes represses PAL1/PAL2 and CHS, revealing metabolite feedback from the flavonol branch.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Indirect PAL regulator via metabolite status.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for engineering flavonol conjugation and avoiding unintended upstream feedback inhibition.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss contributes to feedback repression of PAL and flavonol biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating PAL expression/activity can redirect carbon flux into phenylpropanoids for improved lignin, flavonoid, anthocyanin, and polyphenol traits; useful target for metabolic engineering but requires caution because broad flux redistribution may affect growth and primary metabolism. Stacking with downstream branch genes or regulators may improve trait specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Populus spp.; Phaseolus vulgaris; Petroselinum crispum
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PAL is encoded by multigene families with isoform-specific spatial and temporal expression; generally induced by wounding, UV, heavy metals, low temperature, and nutrient limitation; high expression in lignifying and phenolic-accumulating tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtPAL1 promoter; AtPAL2 promoter; native promoters, inducible/developmentally regulated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL is the first committed and gateway enzyme of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, diverting carbon from primary metabolism into phenolics. 2. PAL catalyzes deamination of L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid and is subject to product inhibition and metabolite feedback regulation. 3. PAL isoforms show tissue-specific and stress-responsive expression, with AtPAL1/2 especially important for flavonoid synthesis and AtPAL1/2/4 closely associated with lignification. 4. PAL proteins are negatively regulated post-translationally through ubiquitination and 26S proteasome-mediated turnover. 5. PAL can associate with ER-localized early phenylpropanoid enzymes, supporting metabolon formation and metabolic channeling.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Vascular tissues/cell walls for lignin; leaves, stems, roots, flowers, anthers, and other phenolic-accumulating tissues depending on branch product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed enzyme; gateway enzyme; rate-determining step supported by cited transgenic tobacco evidence
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry step supplying lignin, flavonoid, anthocyanin, flavonol and related branches
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; partially associated with endoplasmic reticulum/microsomal membrane in some contexts
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Amino acid ammonia-lyase/aminomutase superfamily; MIO-containing enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL occurs as tetramers and may form homo- or heterotetramers; monocot/yeast PALs may also accept tyrosine; phosphorylation remains uncertain/species- or condition-dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis accessions and tobacco systems not consistently specified
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Populus spp.; Phaseolus vulgaris; Petroselinum crispum
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in vascular tissues of roots, leaves, and inflorescence stems; low in root tip and shoot apical meristem; induced by wounding, UVB, nitrogen depletion, heavy metals, low temperature, low phosphate, and other stresses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, development, defense, environmental adaptation, pigmentation, mechanical strength, taste and odor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding; UV/UVB irradiation; heavy metals; low temperature; low nitrogen; low phosphate; high sucrose; elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; promoter-GUS assays; overexpression and co-suppression; enzyme assays; ubiquitination immunoblot; Y2H/BiFC/co-IP for interacting proteins; microsomal fractionation; immunolocalization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the committed precursor pool for all downstream phenylpropanoid end products
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Ubiquitin proteomics in Arabidopsis (Kim et al., 2013a); no accession reported in review
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad perturbation can alter amino acid and carbohydrate metabolism, lignification, defense traits, and growth/development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Non-oxidative deamination of L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid with release of ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes entry flux into phenylpropanoid metabolism and thereby increases downstream lignin, flavonoids, anthocyanins, and related phenolics when active/highly accumulated

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering inducible flavonoid and lignin-related phenylpropanoid output in Arabidopsis and related systems; useful with careful tuning because broad phenylpropanoid flux changes may impact development.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed strongly in inflorescence stems and vascular tissues; induced by nitrogen depletion and environmental stresses; active through much of plant lifetime.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtPAL1 native promoter, developmentally regulated and environmentally responsive
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAL1 is one of the major Arabidopsis PAL isogenes. 2. Together with AtPAL2, it dominates flavonoid synthesis and responds to nitrogen depletion. 3. It also contributes to lignin biosynthesis in stems. 4. AtPAL1 protein is ubiquitinated and targeted by KFB proteins for degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stems; vascular tissues; flavonoid-accumulating tissues
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry with strong contribution to flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pal1 pal2 double mutants are almost devoid of flavonoids despite residual total PAL activity from other isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in vascular tissues of roots, leaves, inflorescence stems; low in root tip and shoot apex; induced by nitrogen depletion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense, pigmentation, stress adaptation, cell wall biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant analysis; promoter-GUS; enzyme activity and metabolite profiling; ubiquitination analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes may affect global phenylpropanoid, amino acid, and carbohydrate metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and contributes to lignification

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing flavonoids and related stress-induced phenolics; may be stacked with AtPAL1 or downstream branch genes for stronger effects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in inflorescence stems and vascular tissues; induced by nitrogen depletion and stresses.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAL2 is a major Arabidopsis PAL isoform. 2. Together with AtPAL1, it is specialized for environmentally triggered flavonoid synthesis. 3. It also contributes to lignin biosynthesis. 4. AtPAL2 is ubiquitinated and negatively regulated through KFB-mediated degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry with strong contribution to flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtPAL1 and AtPAL2 repression occurs in ugt78d1 ugt78d2 flavonol glycosylation mutants and is released in tt4/chs or fls1 backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in vascular tissues; induced by nitrogen depletion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; promoter studies; metabolite profiling; ubiquitination analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes environmentally triggered flavonoid synthesis and contributes to lignin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying lignification, though less emphasized for flavonoid specialization than AtPAL1/2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAL4 is one of the highly expressed PAL isogenes in Arabidopsis stems. 2. Genetic evidence associates AtPAL4 with lignin biosynthesis. 3. It also physically interacts with KFB proteins according to pairwise verification discussed for AtPAL isozymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less emphasized than AtPAL1/2 for flavonoid specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect stem development and wall properties

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PAL protein turnover and thereby phenylpropanoid biosynthesis flux control
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation is a promising route to elevate polyphenols, flavonoids, anthocyanins, and lignin-related phenolics; useful for improving UV tolerance, but must consider possible growth and lignification tradeoffs.
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Kelch repeat-containing F-box protein; SCF-type E3 ligase substrate adaptor
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KFB01 physically interacts with Arabidopsis PAL isozymes. 2. Co-expression with PAL decreases PAL stability and activity. 3. Manipulating KFB expression reciprocally changes PAL abundance and phenylpropanoid accumulation. 4. KFB01 acts as a negative regulator of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis via ubiquitin/26S proteasome-mediated PAL degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High sucrose and UVB suppress KFB expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key post-translational negative regulator of the gateway enzyme PAL
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely involved in metabolite feedback because trans-cinnamate induces KFB expression in unpublished observations discussed in review
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces PAL abundance/activity and lowers phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of PAL proteins rather than transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtPAL1; AtPAL2; AtPAL3; AtPAL4; type-B ARRs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered lignification and carbon allocation; possible effects on cytokinin signaling and plant architecture
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KFB01 also has proposed roles in cytokinin response pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-IP; transient co-expression; MG132 inhibition; Arabidopsis transgenic manipulation; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Carbon status (sucrose); UVB; likely trans-cinnamate feedback
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase complex
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis ubiquitin proteomics referenced; no accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell walls and aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression may enhance phenolic accumulation and UV tolerance; useful target for raising polyphenols.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KFB20 interacts with PAL isozymes. 2. It reduces PAL stability/activity when co-expressed. 3. Its expression correlates inversely with PAL and phenolic accumulation. 4. It acts as a negative regulator of phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High sucrose and UVB suppress expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important PAL turnover factor in vegetative tissues
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Potential feedback involvement downstream of trans-cinnamate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses phenylpropanoid accumulation by destabilizing PAL
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on growth and vascular traits via altered lignification
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly expressed in leaf and stem tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; co-IP; transient co-expression; transgenic manipulation; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaf and stem tissues

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PAL turnover and phenylpropanoid biosynthetic flux
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to elevate polyphenols by reducing PAL degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KFB39, a close homolog of other KFBs, also interacts with Arabidopsis PALs. 2. It is implicated as a negative regulator of PAL stability and pathway activity.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Additional PAL-targeting E3 adaptor broadening control of gateway flux
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely reduces phenylpropanoid accumulation via PAL destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as close homolog interacting with AtPALs.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for elevating phenylpropanoid accumulation through reduced PAL degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KFB50 physically interacts with Arabidopsis PAL isoforms. 2. It negatively affects PAL stability/activity. 3. It is part of the KFB-mediated mechanism controlling phenylpropanoid output.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High sucrose and UVB suppress KFB gene expression collectively
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the decisive post-translational repressors of PAL
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses phenolic accumulation by promoting PAL turnover
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtPAL1; AtPAL2; AtPAL3; AtPAL4; type-B ARRs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potentially links cytokinin signaling and phenylpropanoid regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; co-IP; co-expression destabilization assays; transgenic analysis

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of PAL expression and phenylpropanoid synthesis in anthers
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to drive tissue-specific phenylpropanoid production in reproductive tissues; useful for targeted manipulation rather than whole-plant flux engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtMYBAS1/2 binds the MYB-binding site in the NtPAL1 promoter. 2. It activates NtPAL1 expression in tobacco anthers. 3. It positively regulates phenylpropanoid synthesis in sporophytic tissues such as tapetum, stomium and vascular tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Tissue-specific activator of PAL-driven phenylpropanoid synthesis in anthers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NtPAL1 expression and phenylpropanoid synthesis in anther tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in sporophytic anther tissues rather than general vegetative phenylpropanoid control.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA binding and promoter activation assays reported in cited work
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specific to anthers of young tobacco flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tapetum, stomium, vascular tissues of young anthers

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential floral tissue-specific activator for engineering phenylpropanoid traits in ornamental or reproductive tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmMYB305 is expressed in carpels of young snapdragon flowers. 2. It activates the PAL promoter when co-expressed in tobacco protoplasts. 3. It is implicated in tissue-specific control of phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative floral activator of PAL transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PAL promoter and promotes phenylpropanoid gene expression potential
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is from heterologous tobacco protoplast co-expression.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of PAL and other phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for increasing lignin and related phenylpropanoids, especially in woody/structural biomass engineering; caution due to possible effects on cell wall properties.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tobacco LIM proteins bind AC-rich motif/Pal-box elements. 2. They positively regulate NtPAL and other phenylpropanoid genes. 3. They are implicated in lignin biosynthesis control.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Double LIM zinc finger domain protein
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator coordinating PAL with other lignin-pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PAL expression and lignin-associated phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall strengthening and structural development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NtPAL and other phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter lignification and biomass properties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review refers generally to LIM domain proteins; NtLIM1 is the cited tobacco example.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-binding and functional analysis in cited studies

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative transcriptional control of lignin-related phenylpropanoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation could alter lignification and vascular patterning, but major developmental pleiotropy limits direct breeding use.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mutation of BP upregulates Arabidopsis PAL1, 4CL1, C4H and CAD1. 2. bp mutants show premature lignification of vasculature. 3. BP therefore functions as a negative regulator of PAL and other lignin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses lignin-related phenylpropanoid gene expression and limits premature lignification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes shorter internodes, downward-pointing siliques, and epidermal disorganization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct targeting of PAL promoter remains unresolved.
